businessSummary.oneLiner: Leader in government and enterprise AI data analytics platforms, operating a three-pillar model: Gotham (defense), Foundry (enterprise), and AIP (AI platform)
businessSummary.description: Palantir builds AI data analytics platforms for government and enterprise clients. FY2025 revenue hit $4.48B (+56%), with US commercial revenue surpassing $500M. The company recently expanded its defense AI footprint as Keel joined the Navy ShipOS project, extending AI into submarine production. Over the past three years, the stock has gained 1,860% but has experienced 10+ drawdowns of 20% or more.

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 10x+ the S&P 500 average of 22x. Extreme premium vs software average of 35x

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS of $1.34. Large growth premium but still demanding

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 $4.48B, FY2026E $7.41B (+65.5%). Growth is actually accelerating

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Top-tier among SaaS companies. Achieving high profitability alongside rapid growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: FCF $2.1B. Best-in-class cash generation in software

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Backlog
keyMetrics.interpretation: Per Motley Fool. Multi-year revenue visibility secured

growthDrivers.title: US Navy ShipOS $448M: AI Enters Submarine Production
growthDrivers.description: Keel Holdings joined Palantir and the US Navy on the ShipOS initiative. $448M to deploy Foundry and AIP across naval shipyards, submarine construction, and 100+ suppliers. Defense AI expands from battlefield to manufacturing.

growthDrivers.title: Wedbush $230 + UBS $200: Wall Street Target Rush
growthDrivers.description: Wedbush's Dan Ives maintains $230 Buy (48% upside) citing NVIDIA partnership and AIPCon deals. UBS's Karl Keirstead raised from $180 to $200 (31% upside). Two top analysts simultaneously bullish.

growthDrivers.title: CTO Sankar: '8 Days vs 800 Days' Defense AI Call
growthDrivers.description: CTO Shyam Sankar stated 'the US has only 8 days of weapons for a war with China and needs 800. Deterrence has been lost.' Powerful narrative for defense AI budget increases.

growthDrivers.title: $11.2B Revenue Backlog + $10B Army Contract
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool analysis: $11.2B revenue backlog, $10B direct US Army contract, Anduril $20B subcontract upside. Deep multi-year pipeline.

growthDrivers.title: FY2026 Revenue Forecast $7.41B (+65.5%): Growth Accelerating
growthDrivers.description: From 56% in FY2025 to 65.5% in FY2026. EPS expected to grow 113%. Growth is getting faster, not slower.

growthDrivers.title: NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS Reference Architecture
growthDrivers.description: Palantir software deployed as standard OS on NVIDIA hardware. Positioned to capture sovereign AI infrastructure markets globally.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ year track record in government/defense AI. Security clearance requirements create insurmountable entry barriers. ShipOS extends AI coverage to military manufacturing.

moats.description: Highest-level DoD and intelligence agency clearances. Competitors cannot enter.
AI deployed in submarine production, naval shipyards. Expanding from battlefield to production floor.
Sovereign AI OS + NVIDIA hardware ecosystem. Capturing government AI infrastructure standards globally.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose approach vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse. Expanding into analytics platform territory

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI. Trails Palantir in scale and execution

recentNews.headline: Yahoo Finance: '1,860% in 3 Years, 10+ Drops of 20%+' Volatility Analysis
recentNews.significance: Current pullback fits a recurring pattern. Every prior drawdown recovered, giving long-term investors perspective

recentNews.headline: UBS Raises Target to $200 from $180, Buy Maintained
recentNews.significance: Karl Keirstead cites growth acceleration and AIP expansion

recentNews.headline: Wedbush Dan Ives: $230 Buy, NVIDIA Partnership + AIPCon Tailwinds
recentNews.significance: 48% upside. NVIDIA sovereign AI partnership and AIPCon 9 deal expansion as catalysts

recentNews.headline: CTO Sankar: 'US Has Only 8 Days of Weapons for China War, Needs 800'
recentNews.significance: Strongest public statement on defense AI urgency. Reinforces Palantir's defense revenue narrative

recentNews.headline: Keel Joins Palantir and US Navy in ShipOS Initiative ($448M)
recentNews.significance: AI deployed to accelerate submarine production across 100+ suppliers

analystOpinions.notableComment: Wedbush $230 (+48%), UBS $200 (+31%) simultaneous upgrades. Consensus $194.05 from 28 analysts. 3-year track record of recovering from every 20%+ drawdown.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation
risks.description: P/E 221x is 10x the S&P 500 average. Any growth miss could trigger a sharp decline. The stock has experienced 10+ drops of 20% or more in three years.

risks.title: Insider Selling Continues
risks.description: CEO Karp and insiders sell roughly $6M daily. While under 10b5-1 plans, it raises questions about executive conviction.

risks.title: Government Revenue Concentration
risks.description: Significant revenue share from US government contracts. Administration changes or budget cuts create direct exposure.

risks.title: Stock Dilution
risks.description: SBC (stock-based compensation) dilutes roughly 5% annually. Ongoing shareholder value erosion.

risks.title: AI Market Competition
risks.description: Microsoft, AWS, Google expanding enterprise AI investments. Long-term market share defense required.

buyReasons.title: Wedbush $230 + UBS $200: Top Analysts Simultaneously Bullish
buyReasons.rationale: 48% and 31% upside from two top-rated analysts. NVIDIA partnership and AIPCon deal expansion as primary catalysts. Wall Street conviction is building.

buyReasons.title: ShipOS $448M: AI Expands to Military Manufacturing
buyReasons.rationale: Submarine production, naval shipyards, 100+ suppliers getting AI. Defense AI moves beyond battlefield to production floor.

buyReasons.title: $11.2B Backlog + $10B Army + $20B Anduril Subcontract
buyReasons.rationale: Extremely deep pipeline securing multi-year growth visibility.

buyReasons.title: 3-Year 1,860%: Recovery Track Record After Every Drawdown
buyReasons.rationale: 10+ drops of 20% or more over three years, each followed by recovery. Current 26% pullback from highs fits the historical pattern.

overallOpinion: Wedbush ($230) and UBS ($200) raised targets simultaneously. ShipOS $448M, $11.2B backlog, 65.5% growth forecast. CTO Sankar's '8 days of weapons' statement drives the defense AI narrative. Both Wall Street and management are fully committed to the growth story.
But at P/E 221x, good news alone isn't enough. The stock fell 1.5% despite ShipOS. Yahoo Finance reminded us: 1,860% in 3 years but 10+ drops of 20%+. This is the stock's nature.

Trading just below the 50-day MA ($158) with RSI 46. Down 26% from the $207 high. Historically, every such drawdown recovered, but the valuation burden suggests dollar-cost averaging in the $140-150 range is more prudent than a full entry here.

investmentVerdict.summary: Wedbush $230 and UBS $200 upgrades reflect growing Wall Street conviction. $11.2B backlog and 65.5% growth are rock-solid. However, P/E 221x and daily CEO selling ($6M) warrant dollar-cost averaging around $140-150 rather than a full position at current levels.

x.hook: Wedbush $230 + UBS $200: Wall Street goes all-in on Palantir
x.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%). Wedbush $230 (+48%), UBS $200 (+31%) both raised. ShipOS $448M, $11.2B backlog. But at P/E 221x, good news wasn't enough.

1/3
thread:
1,860% in 3 years but 10+ drops of 20%+. That's the PLTR experience. CTO says 'US has 8 days of weapons.' The growth story is real. The volatility is too.

2/3

Down 26% from highs, below 50-day MA, RSI 46. Historically recovers from every drawdown. DCA into $140-150 range. Consensus $194 (+27%).

3/3

threads.hook: Wedbush $230, UBS $200: Wall Street simultaneously raises Palantir targets
threads.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%)

Wedbush's Dan Ives ($230, +48%) and UBS's Karl Keirstead ($200, +31%) both raised targets. ShipOS $448M, $11.2B backlog, 65.5% growth forecast. Wall Street conviction is building.

But at P/E 221x, good news drew a 1.5% decline. 1,860% in 3 years, 10+ drops of 20%+. Volatility is in Palantir's DNA.

Down 26% from highs. Every prior drawdown recovered, but DCA into $140-150 is more comfortable than a full entry here.

Consensus $194.05 (+27%), 16/28 Buy
Verdict: Buy (DCA, 6 months to 1 year)

Wedbush's Dan Ives ($230, +48%) and UBS's Karl Keirstead ($200, +31%) both raised targets. ShipOS $448M, $11.2B backlog, 65.5% growth forecast. Wall Street conviction is building.

But at P/E 221x, good news drew a 1.5% decline. 1,860% in 3 years, 10+ drops of 20%+. Volatility is in Palantir's DNA.

witty.hook: Palantir: Wall Street says buy, CEO says sell ($6M daily)
witty.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%)

Wedbush $230, UBS $200. Two top analysts raised targets on the same day. Meanwhile, Karp keeps selling $6M daily. You'd think they'd at least sync calendars.

CTO warns about '8 days of weapons' against China. That's both terrifying and great for business. $11.2B backlog proves the point. 1,860% in 3 years.

P/E 221x and good news means a red day. That's just PLTR culture. $140 and we can have a real conversation.

empathy.hook: Palantir holders: analyst upgrades plus a red close. The struggle is real.
empathy.text:
$PLTR (Mar 19) $152.77 (-1.5%)

Wedbush $230, UBS $200. Both raised. ShipOS $448M. All positive. Yet the stock fell. When you hold a P/E 221x stock, this is the tax you pay.

But 1,860% in 3 years happened through 10+ drawdowns of 20%+. Every time, it came back. This 26% pullback fits the pattern.

$11.2B backlog, 65.5% growth. The business is at its strongest ever. DCA helps you stay in the game when days like this make you want to quit.
